news_results.title: Iran strikes were launched without approval from Congress, deeply dividing lawmakers
news_results.description: Top lawmakers were notified about the operation shortly before it was launched, but the White House did not seek authorization from Congress to carry out the strikes.

news_results.title: Are the US and Israel at war with Iran if Congress hasn't declared it?
news_results.description: The U.S. Constitution gives Congress the sole power to declare war, but since 1973, every president has used the War Powers Act.

news_results.description: Both the House and Senate, where the president's Republican Party has a slim majority, had already drafted such resolutions long before the strikes Saturday.
